{"file_path": "/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/cmd/server/main.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"context"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"fleetcommerce/internal/alerts"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/app"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/audit"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/auth"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/cart"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/catalog"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/db"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/handlers"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/middleware"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/views"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/imports"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/metrics"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/notifications"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/orders"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/rbac"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/scheduler"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

func main() {
	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stdout, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: slog.LevelInfo})))

	cfg := app.LoadConfig()

	// Database
	pool, err := db.Connect(cfg.DatabaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		slog.Error("database connection failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	defer pool.Close()

	// Migrations
	if err := db.RunMigrations(cfg.DatabaseURL); err != nil {
		slog.Error("migration failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	// Services
	auditSvc := audit.NewService(pool)
	authSvc := auth.NewService(pool)
	rbacSvc := rbac.NewService(pool)
	catalogRepo := catalog.NewRepository(pool)
	catalogSvc := catalog.NewService(catalogRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	cartRepo := cart.NewRepository(pool)
	cartSvc := cart.NewService(cartRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	orderRepo := orders.NewRepository(pool)
	orderSvc := orders.NewService(orderRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	notifSvc := notifications.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	alertSvc := alerts.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	metricSvc := metrics.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	importSvc := imports.NewService(pool, auditSvc)

	// Renderer
	renderer := views.NewRenderer(true)

	// Handlers
	h := handlers.New(authSvc, catalogSvc, cartSvc, orderSvc, notifSvc, alertSvc, metricSvc, auditSvc, importSvc, renderer, cfg.UploadsDir, cfg.MaxUploadBytes)

	// Ensure directories
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.UploadsDir, 0755)
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.ExportsDir, 0755)

	// Router
	gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)
	r := gin.New()
	r.Use(gin.Recovery())
	r.MaxMultipartMemory = cfg.MaxUploadBytes

	// Static files
	r.Static("/static", "./web/static")
	r.Static("/uploads", cfg.UploadsDir)

	// CSRF
	r.Use(middleware.CSRFMiddleware())

	// Public routes
	r.GET("/login", h.LoginPage)
	r.POST("/login", h.LoginPost)

	// Authenticated routes
	authed := r.Group("/")
	authed.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		authed.POST("/logout", h.Logout)
		authed.GET("/", h.DashboardPage)

		// Catalog pages
		authed.GET("/catalog", h.CatalogPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/new", h.CatalogEditPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/import", h.ImportModal)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id", h.CatalogDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id/edit", h.CatalogEditPage)

		// Cart pages
		authed.GET("/cart", h.CartPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id", h.CartDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id/merge-modal", h.MergeCartModal)

		// Order pages
		authed.GET("/orders", h.OrdersPage)
		authed.GET("/orders/:id", h.OrderDetailPage)

		// Notification pages
		authed.GET("/notifications", h.NotificationsPage)

		// Alert pages
		authed.GET("/alerts", h.AlertsPage)
		authed.GET("/alerts/:id/close-modal", h.CloseAlertModal)

		// Metric pages
		authed.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricsPage)
		authed.GET("/metrics/:id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricDetailPage)

		// Audit pages
		authed.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditPage)
	}

	// API routes
	api := r.Group("/api")
	api.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		api.GET("/me", h.GetMe)
		api.GET("/dashboard/summary", h.DashboardSummaryAPI)

		// Catalog API
		api.GET("/catalog/brands", h.ListBrandsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/series", h.ListSeriesAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models", h.ListModelsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models/:id", h.GetModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.CreateModelAPI)
		api.PUT("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/publish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.PublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/unpublish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.UnpublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/media", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMediaUpload), h.UploadMediaAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/export.csv", h.ExportCatalogCSV)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.ImportCatalogCSV)
		api.GET("/catalog/imports/:job_id", h.GetImportJob)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports/:job_id/commit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.CommitImportJob)

		// Cart API
		api.GET("/carts", h.ListCartsAPI)
		api.POST("/carts", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.CreateCartAPI)
		api.GET("/carts/:id", h.GetCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/items", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.AddCartItemAPI)
		api.PUT("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.UpdateCartItemAPI)
		api.DELETE("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.DeleteCartItemAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/merge", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartMerge), h.MergeCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/revalidate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.RevalidateCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/checkout", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderCreate), h.CheckoutCartAPI)

		// Order API
		api.GET("/orders", h.ListOrdersAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id", h.GetOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/notes", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderNotes), h.AddOrderNoteAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/payment-recorded", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderPayment), h.RecordPaymentAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/transition", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderTransition), h.TransitionOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/split", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderSplit), h.SplitOrderAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id/timeline", h.OrderTimelineAPI)

		// Notification API
		api.GET("/notifications", h.ListNotificationsAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/:id/read", h.MarkNotificationReadAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/bulk-read", h.BulkMarkReadAPI)
		api.GET("/announcements", h.ListAnnouncementsAPI)
		api.GET("/notification-preferences", h.GetPreferencesAPI)
		api.PUT("/notification-preferences", h.UpdatePreferencesAPI)
		api.GET("/export-queue", h.ListExportQueueAPI)

		// Alert API
		api.GET("/alerts", h.ListAlertsAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/claim", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ClaimAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/process", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ProcessAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/close", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.CloseAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/evaluate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.EvaluateAlertsAPI)

		// Metric API
		api.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.ListMetricsAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricWrite), h.CreateMetricAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/impact-analysis", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ImpactAnalysisAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/activate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ActivateMetricAPI)
		api.GET("/metrics/:id/lineage", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricLineageAPI)

		// Audit API
		api.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.ListAuditAPI)
		api.GET("/audit/:entity_type/:entity_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditByEntityAPI)
	}

	// Scheduler
	if cfg.SchedulerEnabled {
		sched := scheduler.New(orderSvc, alertSvc, notifSvc, cfg.CutoffInterval, cfg.AlertInterval, cfg.ExportRetryInt)
		sched.Start()
		defer sched.Stop()
	}

	// Server
	srv := &http.Server{
		Addr:    ":" + cfg.Port,
		Handler: r,
	}

	go func() {
		slog.Info("server starting", "port", cfg.Port)
		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
			slog.Error("server error", "error", err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}
	}()

	// Graceful shutdown
	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
	<-quit

	slog.Info("shutting down server...")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	srv.Shutdown(ctx)
	slog.Info("server stopped")
}
"}

{"file_path": "/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/cmd/server/main.go", "content": "package main

import (
	"context"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"fleetcommerce/internal/alerts"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/app"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/audit"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/auth"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/cart"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/catalog"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/db"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/handlers"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/middleware"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/http/views"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/imports"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/metrics"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/notifications"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/orders"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/rbac"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/scheduler"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

func main() {
	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stdout, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: slog.LevelInfo})))

	cfg := app.LoadConfig()

	// Database
	pool, err := db.Connect(cfg.DatabaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		slog.Error("database connection failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	defer pool.Close()

	// Migrations
	if err := db.RunMigrations(cfg.DatabaseURL); err != nil {
		slog.Error("migration failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	// Services
	auditSvc := audit.NewService(pool)
	authSvc := auth.NewService(pool)
	rbacSvc := rbac.NewService(pool)
	catalogRepo := catalog.NewRepository(pool)
	catalogSvc := catalog.NewService(catalogRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	cartRepo := cart.NewRepository(pool)
	cartSvc := cart.NewService(cartRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	orderRepo := orders.NewRepository(pool)
	orderSvc := orders.NewService(orderRepo, pool, auditSvc)
	notifSvc := notifications.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	alertSvc := alerts.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	metricSvc := metrics.NewService(pool, auditSvc)
	importSvc := imports.NewService(pool, auditSvc)

	// Renderer
	renderer := views.NewRenderer(true)

	// Handlers
	h := handlers.New(authSvc, catalogSvc, cartSvc, orderSvc, notifSvc, alertSvc, metricSvc, auditSvc, importSvc, renderer, cfg.UploadsDir, cfg.MaxUploadBytes)

	// Ensure directories
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.UploadsDir, 0755)
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.ExportsDir, 0755)

	// Router
	gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)
	r := gin.New()
	r.Use(gin.Recovery())
	r.MaxMultipartMemory = cfg.MaxUploadBytes

	// Static files
	r.Static("/static", "./web/static")
	r.Static("/uploads", cfg.UploadsDir)

	// CSRF
	r.Use(middleware.CSRFMiddleware())

	// Public routes
	r.GET("/login", h.LoginPage)
	r.POST("/login", h.LoginPost)

	// Authenticated routes
	authed := r.Group("/")
	authed.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		authed.POST("/logout", h.Logout)
		authed.GET("/", h.DashboardPage)

		// Catalog pages
		authed.GET("/catalog", h.CatalogPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/new", h.CatalogEditPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/import", h.ImportModal)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id", h.CatalogDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id/edit", h.CatalogEditPage)

		// Cart pages
		authed.GET("/cart", h.CartPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id", h.CartDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id/merge-modal", h.MergeCartModal)

		// Order pages
		authed.GET("/orders", h.OrdersPage)
		authed.GET("/orders/:id", h.OrderDetailPage)

		// Notification pages
		authed.GET("/notifications", h.NotificationsPage)

		// Alert pages
		authed.GET("/alerts", h.AlertsPage)
		authed.GET("/alerts/:id/close-modal", h.CloseAlertModal)

		// Metric pages
		authed.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricsPage)
		authed.GET("/metrics/:id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricDetailPage)

		// Audit pages
		authed.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditPage)
	}

	// API routes
	api := r.Group("/api")
	api.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		api.GET("/me", h.GetMe)
		api.GET("/dashboard/summary", h.DashboardSummaryAPI)

		// Catalog API
		api.GET("/catalog/brands", h.ListBrandsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/series", h.ListSeriesAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models", h.ListModelsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models/:id", h.GetModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.CreateModelAPI)
		api.PUT("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/publish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.PublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/unpublish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.UnpublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/media", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMediaUpload), h.UploadMediaAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/export.csv", h.ExportCatalogCSV)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.ImportCatalogCSV)
		api.GET("/catalog/imports/:job_id", h.GetImportJob)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports/:job_id/commit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.CommitImportJob)

		// Cart API
		api.GET("/carts", h.ListCartsAPI)
		api.POST("/carts", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.CreateCartAPI)
		api.GET("/carts/:id", h.GetCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/items", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.AddCartItemAPI)
		api.PUT("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.UpdateCartItemAPI)
		api.DELETE("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.DeleteCartItemAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/merge", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartMerge), h.MergeCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/revalidate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.RevalidateCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/checkout", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderCreate), h.CheckoutCartAPI)

		// Order API
		api.GET("/orders", h.ListOrdersAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id", h.GetOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/notes", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderNotes), h.AddOrderNoteAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/payment-recorded", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderPayment), h.RecordPaymentAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/transition", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderTransition), h.TransitionOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/split", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderSplit), h.SplitOrderAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id/timeline", h.OrderTimelineAPI)

		// Notification API
		api.GET("/notifications", h.ListNotificationsAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/:id/read", h.MarkNotificationReadAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/bulk-read", h.BulkMarkReadAPI)
		api.GET("/announcements", h.ListAnnouncementsAPI)
		api.GET("/notification-preferences", h.GetPreferencesAPI)
		api.PUT("/notification-preferences", h.UpdatePreferencesAPI)
		api.GET("/export-queue", h.ListExportQueueAPI)

		// Alert API
		api.GET("/alerts", h.ListAlertsAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/claim", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ClaimAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/process", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ProcessAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/close", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.CloseAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/evaluate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.EvaluateAlertsAPI)

		// Metric API
		api.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.ListMetricsAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricWrite), h.CreateMetricAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/impact-analysis", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ImpactAnalysisAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/activate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ActivateMetricAPI)
		api.GET("/metrics/:id/lineage", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricLineageAPI)

		// Audit API
		api.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.ListAuditAPI)
		api.GET("/audit/:entity_type/:entity_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditByEntityAPI)
	}

	// Scheduler
	if cfg.SchedulerEnabled {
		sched := scheduler.New(orderSvc, alertSvc, notifSvc, cfg.CutoffInterval, cfg.AlertInterval, cfg.ExportRetryInt)
		sched.Start()
		defer sched.Stop()
	}

	// Server
	srv := &http.Server{
		Addr:    ":" + cfg.Port,
		Handler: r,
	}

	go func() {
		slog.Info("server starting", "port", cfg.Port)
		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
			slog.Error("server error", "error", err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}
	}()

	// Graceful shutdown
	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
	<-quit

	slog.Info("shutting down server...")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	srv.Shutdown(ctx)
	slog.Info("server stopped")
}
"}

{"file_path": "/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/README.md", "content": "# FleetCommerce Operations Hub

An offline-first dealership parts and vehicle catalog workflow system built with Go, Gin, PostgreSQL, and HTMX.

## Architecture

```
cmd/server/          - Main server entry point
cmd/seed/            - Database seed tool (generates proper password hashes)
internal/
  app/               - Configuration
  auth/              - Authentication (sessions, login/logout)
  rbac/              - Role-based access control (permission matrix)
  catalog/           - Vehicle catalog (brands, series, models, versions)
  media/             - Media upload handling
  cart/              - Shopping carts, items, validation, merge
  orders/            - Orders, state machine, notes, split/backorder
  notifications/     - Notification center, templates, export queues
  alerts/            - Inventory alerts, rules, lifecycle
  metrics/           - Metric framework, versioning, impact analysis, lineage
  audit/             - Immutable audit logging
  imports/           - CSV import/export with two-phase commit
  scheduler/         - Background jobs (cutoff, alerts, export retry)
  crypto/            - Argon2id hashing, AES-GCM encryption
  masking/           - Phone/email masking
  db/                - Database connection, embedded migrations
  http/
    handlers/        - HTTP handlers (REST + HTML pages)
    middleware/      - Auth, RBAC, CSRF middleware
    views/           - Template renderer + embedded HTML templates
web/
  static/css/        - Stylesheet
  uploads/           - Uploaded media files
  exports/           - Export queue output
migrations/          - (embedded in internal/db/migrations)
testdata/            - Sample CSV files
```

## Prerequisites

- Go 1.22+
- PostgreSQL 14+

## Setup

### 1. Create PostgreSQL database

```bash
createdb fleetcommerce
createuser fleet -P  # password: fleet
psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE fleetcommerce TO fleet;"
```

Or with Docker:

```bash
docker run -d --name fleetcommerce-db \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=fleet \
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=fleet \
  -e POSTGRES_DB=fleetcommerce \
  -p 5432:5432 postgres:16
```

### 2. Configure environment (optional)

Defaults work out of the box for local development:

```bash
export DATABASE_URL="postgres://fleet:fleet@localhost:5432/fleetcommerce?sslmode=disable"
export SESSION_SECRET="change-me-in-production-32bytes!"
export ENCRYPTION_KEY="0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef"
export APP_PORT=8080
```

### 3. Run migrations and start server

```bash
go run ./cmd/server
```

Migrations run automatically on startup.

### 4. Seed demo data with proper password hashes

```bash
go run ./cmd/seed
```

This updates the seeded user passwords to proper Argon2id hashes.

### 5. Access the application

Open http://localhost:8080

## Demo Credentials

| Username    | Password      | Role              |
|-------------|---------------|-------------------|
| admin       | password123   | Administrator     |
| inventory   | password123   | Inventory Manager |
| sales       | password123   | Sales Associate   |
| auditor     | password123   | Auditor           |

**Note:** Run `go run ./cmd/seed` after first startup to set proper password hashes.

## Configuration

| Env Variable           | Default                     | Description                    |
|------------------------|-----------------------------|--------------------------------|
| APP_PORT               | 8080                        | Server port                    |
| DATABASE_URL           | postgres://fleet:fleet@...  | PostgreSQL connection string   |
| SESSION_SECRET         | change-me...                | Session cookie signing key     |
| ENCRYPTION_KEY         | 0123456789abcdef...         | AES-GCM key for encryption     |
| UPLOADS_DIR            | ./web/uploads               | File upload directory          |
| EXPORTS_DIR            | ./web/exports               | Export queue output directory   |
| MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES       | 26214400 (25MB)             | Max upload file size           |
| SCHEDULER_ENABLED      | true                        | Enable background scheduler    |
| CUTOFF_INTERVAL_SEC    | 60                          | Order cutoff check interval    |
| ALERT_INTERVAL_SEC     | 900                         | Alert evaluation interval      |
| EXPORT_RETRY_INTERVAL_SEC | 300                      | Export retry interval          |

## Key Features

### Vehicle Catalog
- Brand > Series > Model hierarchical browsing
- Draft/publish/unpublish workflow with versioned content
- Media upload with MIME validation, size limits, SHA-256 fingerprinting
- CSV import with two-phase preview/commit and row-level validation
- CSV export

### Cart Management
- Create carts per customer account
- Add/remove/update line items
- Automatic validation against catalog state (discontinued, unpublished, out of stock)
- Cart merge by customer account with deduplication
- Checkout converts cart to order

### Order Management
- Server-enforced state machine: created -> payment_recorded -> picking -> arrival -> pickup/delivery -> completed
- Automatic cutoff at 30 minutes if payment not recorded
- Order notes (internal, picking, delivery)
- Order split for backorder handling
- Visual timeline of state transitions
- Payment recording with permission gate

### Notification Center
- In-app notifications with read/unread state
- Bulk mark-as-read
- System announcements with priority levels
- User subscription preferences
- Template rendering with variable substitution
- Local export queues (email/SMS/webhook simulation)

### Inventory Alerts
- Configurable alert rules (low stock, overstock, near expiry)
- Scheduled evaluation every 15 minutes
- Claim/process/close lifecycle
- Mandatory resolution notes on close

### Metric Framework
- Versioned metric definitions
- Dependency tracking between metrics
- Impact analysis before activation
- Lineage visualization (metric -> metric, chart -> metric)
- Metric-level permissions

### Security
- Argon2id password hashing
- AES-GCM encryption at rest for sensitive fields
- Phone number masking in UI
- CSRF protection on forms
- Role-based permission matrix
- Session-based authentication

### Audit Trail
- Immutable append-only audit log
- Captures entity type, ID, action, actor, before/after JSON, metadata
- Covers: catalog mutations, cart operations, order transitions, alerts, metrics, imports

## Running Tests

```bash
go test ./...
```

## Known Simplifications

- "Email/SMS/webhook" exports are stored locally, not actually sent
- Metric framework stores definitions and governance metadata, not a full BI engine
- Template rendering uses simple string replacement, not full Go templates
- File uploads stored on local filesystem
- Single-server architecture (no distributed scheduling)
- Session cleanup runs as part of the scheduler, not a separate service
"}

## Project Stats
- **57 source files** across Go, SQL, HTML, CSS, CSV, and Markdown

8. **Notifications** - In-app notifications, announcements, read/unread, bulk mark-read, templates with variable substitution, local export queues

30		}

func main() {

	cfg := app.LoadConfig()

	// Wait for database to be reachable (critical for docker compose)
	var pool *db.PoolWrapper
	var err error
	for attempt := 1; attempt <= 30; attempt++ {
		pool, err = tryConnect(cfg.DatabaseURL)
		if err == nil {
			break
		}
		slog.Warn("database not ready, retrying...", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
	}
	if err != nil {
		slog.Error("database connection failed after retries", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	pgpool := pool.Pool
	defer pgpool.Close()

	// Migrations
	if err := db.RunMigrations(cfg.DatabaseURL); err != nil {
		slog.Error("migration failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	// Seed demo data (idempotent)
	db.RunSeeds(context.Background(), pgpool, cfg.EncryptionKey)

	authSvc := auth.NewService(pgpool)

	// Ensure directories
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.UploadsDir, 0755)
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.ExportsDir, 0755)

	// Router
	gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)

	r.Use(gin.Recovery())
	r.MaxMultipartMemory = cfg.MaxUploadBytes

	r.Use(middleware.CSRFMiddleware())

	authed := r.Group("/")
	authed.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))

		authed.GET("/catalog/:id/edit", h.CatalogEditPage)

		authed.GET("/cart/:id/merge-modal", h.MergeCartModal)

		api.POST("/catalog/imports/:job_id/commit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.CommitImportJob)

		api.POST("/orders/:id/notes", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderNotes), h.AddOrderNoteAPI)

		api.GET("/orders/:id/timeline", h.OrderTimelineAPI)

		// Notification API

		api.POST("/notifications/:id/read", h.MarkNotificationReadAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/bulk-read", h.BulkMarkReadAPI)

		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {

			os.Exit(1)
		}
	}()

	// Graceful shutdown
	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
	<-quit

	slog.Info("shutting down server...")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	srv.Shutdown(ctx)

func tryConnect(databaseURL string) (*db.PoolWrapper, error) {
	pool, err := db.Connect(databaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &db.PoolWrapper{Pool: pool}, nil
}

	"fleetcommerce/internal/orders"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/rbac"
	"fleetcommerce/internal/scheduler"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

func main() {
	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stdout, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: slog.LevelInfo})))

	cfg := app.LoadConfig()

	// Wait for database to be reachable (critical for docker compose)
	var pool *db.PoolWrapper
	var err error
	for attempt := 1; attempt <= 30; attempt++ {
		pool, err = tryConnect(cfg.DatabaseURL)
		if err == nil {
			break
		}
		slog.Warn("database not ready, retrying...", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
	}
	if err != nil {
		slog.Error("database connection failed after retries", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	pgpool := pool.Pool
	defer pgpool.Close()

	// Migrations
	if err := db.RunMigrations(cfg.DatabaseURL); err != nil {
		slog.Error("migration failed", "error", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	// Seed demo data (idempotent)
	db.RunSeeds(context.Background(), pgpool, cfg.EncryptionKey)

	// Services
	auditSvc := audit.NewService(pgpool)
	authSvc := auth.NewService(pgpool)
	rbacSvc := rbac.NewService(pgpool)
	catalogRepo := catalog.NewRepository(pgpool)
	catalogSvc := catalog.NewService(catalogRepo, pgpool, auditSvc)
	cartRepo := cart.NewRepository(pgpool)
	cartSvc := cart.NewService(cartRepo, pgpool, auditSvc)
	orderRepo := orders.NewRepository(pgpool)
	orderSvc := orders.NewService(orderRepo, pgpool, auditSvc)
	notifSvc := notifications.NewService(pgpool, auditSvc)
	alertSvc := alerts.NewService(pgpool, auditSvc)
	metricSvc := metrics.NewService(pgpool, auditSvc)
	importSvc := imports.NewService(pgpool, auditSvc)

	// Renderer
	renderer := views.NewRenderer(true)

	// Handlers
	h := handlers.New(authSvc, catalogSvc, cartSvc, orderSvc, notifSvc, alertSvc, metricSvc, auditSvc, importSvc, renderer, cfg.UploadsDir, cfg.MaxUploadBytes)

	// Ensure directories
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.UploadsDir, 0755)
	os.MkdirAll(cfg.ExportsDir, 0755)

	// Router
	gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)
	r := gin.New()
	r.Use(gin.Recovery())
	r.MaxMultipartMemory = cfg.MaxUploadBytes

	// Static files
	r.Static("/static", "./web/static")
	r.Static("/uploads", cfg.UploadsDir)

	// CSRF
	r.Use(middleware.CSRFMiddleware())

	// Public routes
	r.GET("/login", h.LoginPage)
	r.POST("/login", h.LoginPost)

	// Authenticated routes
	authed := r.Group("/")
	authed.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		authed.POST("/logout", h.Logout)
		authed.GET("/", h.DashboardPage)

		// Catalog pages
		authed.GET("/catalog", h.CatalogPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/new", h.CatalogEditPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/import", h.ImportModal)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id", h.CatalogDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id/edit", h.CatalogEditPage)

		// Cart pages
		authed.GET("/cart", h.CartPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id", h.CartDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id/merge-modal", h.MergeCartModal)

		// Order pages
		authed.GET("/orders", h.OrdersPage)
		authed.GET("/orders/:id", h.OrderDetailPage)

		// Notification pages
		authed.GET("/notifications", h.NotificationsPage)

		// Alert pages
		authed.GET("/alerts", h.AlertsPage)
		authed.GET("/alerts/:id/close-modal", h.CloseAlertModal)

		// Metric pages
		authed.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricsPage)
		authed.GET("/metrics/:id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricDetailPage)

		// Audit pages
		authed.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditPage)
	}

	// API routes
	api := r.Group("/api")
	api.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		api.GET("/me", h.GetMe)
		api.GET("/dashboard/summary", h.DashboardSummaryAPI)

		// Catalog API
		api.GET("/catalog/brands", h.ListBrandsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/series", h.ListSeriesAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models", h.ListModelsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models/:id", h.GetModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.CreateModelAPI)
		api.PUT("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/publish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.PublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/unpublish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.UnpublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/media", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMediaUpload), h.UploadMediaAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/export.csv", h.ExportCatalogCSV)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.ImportCatalogCSV)
		api.GET("/catalog/imports/:job_id", h.GetImportJob)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports/:job_id/commit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.CommitImportJob)

		// Cart API
		api.GET("/carts", h.ListCartsAPI)
		api.POST("/carts", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.CreateCartAPI)
		api.GET("/carts/:id", h.GetCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/items", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.AddCartItemAPI)
		api.PUT("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.UpdateCartItemAPI)
		api.DELETE("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.DeleteCartItemAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/merge", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartMerge), h.MergeCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/revalidate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.RevalidateCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/checkout", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderCreate), h.CheckoutCartAPI)

		// Order API
		api.GET("/orders", h.ListOrdersAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id", h.GetOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/notes", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderNotes), h.AddOrderNoteAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/payment-recorded", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderPayment), h.RecordPaymentAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/transition", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderTransition), h.TransitionOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/split", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderSplit), h.SplitOrderAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id/timeline", h.OrderTimelineAPI)

		// Notification API
		api.GET("/notifications", h.ListNotificationsAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/:id/read", h.MarkNotificationReadAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/bulk-read", h.BulkMarkReadAPI)
		api.GET("/announcements", h.ListAnnouncementsAPI)
		api.GET("/notification-preferences", h.GetPreferencesAPI)
		api.PUT("/notification-preferences", h.UpdatePreferencesAPI)
		api.GET("/export-queue", h.ListExportQueueAPI)

		// Alert API
		api.GET("/alerts", h.ListAlertsAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/claim", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ClaimAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/process", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ProcessAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/close", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.CloseAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/evaluate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.EvaluateAlertsAPI)

		// Metric API
		api.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.ListMetricsAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricWrite), h.CreateMetricAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/impact-analysis", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ImpactAnalysisAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/activate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ActivateMetricAPI)
		api.GET("/metrics/:id/lineage", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricLineageAPI)

		// Audit API
		api.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.ListAuditAPI)
		api.GET("/audit/:entity_type/:entity_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditByEntityAPI)
	}

	// Scheduler
	if cfg.SchedulerEnabled {
		sched := scheduler.New(orderSvc, alertSvc, notifSvc, cfg.CutoffInterval, cfg.AlertInterval, cfg.ExportRetryInt)
		sched.Start()
		defer sched.Stop()
	}

	// Server
	srv := &http.Server{
		Addr:    ":" + cfg.Port,
		Handler: r,
	}

	go func() {
		slog.Info("server starting", "port", cfg.Port)
		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
			slog.Error("server error", "error", err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}
	}()

	// Graceful shutdown
	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
	<-quit

	slog.Info("shutting down server...")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	srv.Shutdown(ctx)
	slog.Info("server stopped")
}

func tryConnect(databaseURL string) (*db.PoolWrapper, error) {
	pool, err := db.Connect(databaseURL)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &db.PoolWrapper{Pool: pool}, nil
}
"}

	r.Use(middleware.CSRFMiddleware())

	// Public routes
	r.GET("/login", h.LoginPage)
	r.POST("/login", h.LoginPost)

	// Authenticated routes
	authed := r.Group("/")
	authed.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		authed.POST("/logout", h.Logout)
		authed.GET("/", h.DashboardPage)

		authed.GET("/catalog", h.CatalogPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/new", h.CatalogEditPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/import", h.ImportModal)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id", h.CatalogDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/catalog/:id/edit", h.CatalogEditPage)

		authed.GET("/cart", h.CartPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id", h.CartDetailPage)
		authed.GET("/cart/:id/merge-modal", h.MergeCartModal)

		authed.GET("/orders", h.OrdersPage)
		authed.GET("/orders/:id", h.OrderDetailPage)

		authed.GET("/notifications", h.NotificationsPage)

		authed.GET("/alerts", h.AlertsPage)
		authed.GET("/alerts/:id/close-modal", h.CloseAlertModal)

		authed.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricsPage)
		authed.GET("/metrics/:id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricDetailPage)

		authed.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditPage)
	}

	// API routes
	api := r.Group("/api")
	api.Use(middleware.AuthMiddleware(authSvc, rbacSvc))
	{
		api.GET("/me", h.GetMe)
		api.GET("/dashboard/summary", h.DashboardSummaryAPI)

		api.GET("/catalog/brands", h.ListBrandsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/series", h.ListSeriesAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models", h.ListModelsAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/models/:id", h.GetModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.CreateModelAPI)
		api.PUT("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/draft", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogWrite), h.UpdateDraftAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/publish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.PublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/unpublish", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogPublish), h.UnpublishModelAPI)
		api.POST("/catalog/models/:id/media", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMediaUpload), h.UploadMediaAPI)
		api.GET("/catalog/export.csv", h.ExportCatalogCSV)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.ImportCatalogCSV)
		api.GET("/catalog/imports/:job_id", h.GetImportJob)
		api.POST("/catalog/imports/:job_id/commit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCatalogImport), h.CommitImportJob)

		api.GET("/carts", h.ListCartsAPI)
		api.POST("/carts", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.CreateCartAPI)
		api.GET("/carts/:id", h.GetCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/items", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.AddCartItemAPI)
		api.PUT("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.UpdateCartItemAPI)
		api.DELETE("/carts/:id/items/:item_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.DeleteCartItemAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/merge", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartMerge), h.MergeCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/revalidate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite), h.RevalidateCartAPI)
		api.POST("/carts/:id/checkout", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderCreate), h.CheckoutCartAPI)

		api.GET("/orders", h.ListOrdersAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id", h.GetOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/notes", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderNotes), h.AddOrderNoteAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/payment-recorded", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderPayment), h.RecordPaymentAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/transition", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderTransition), h.TransitionOrderAPI)
		api.POST("/orders/:id/split", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderSplit), h.SplitOrderAPI)
		api.GET("/orders/:id/timeline", h.OrderTimelineAPI)

		api.GET("/notifications", h.ListNotificationsAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/:id/read", h.MarkNotificationReadAPI)
		api.POST("/notifications/bulk-read", h.BulkMarkReadAPI)
		api.GET("/announcements", h.ListAnnouncementsAPI)
		api.GET("/notification-preferences", h.GetPreferencesAPI)
		api.PUT("/notification-preferences", h.UpdatePreferencesAPI)
		api.GET("/export-queue", h.ListExportQueueAPI)

		api.GET("/alerts", h.ListAlertsAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/claim", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ClaimAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/process", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.ProcessAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/:id/close", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.CloseAlertAPI)
		api.POST("/alerts/evaluate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage), h.EvaluateAlertsAPI)

		api.GET("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.ListMetricsAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricWrite), h.CreateMetricAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/impact-analysis", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ImpactAnalysisAPI)
		api.POST("/metrics/:id/activate", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricActivate), h.ActivateMetricAPI)
		api.GET("/metrics/:id/lineage", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead), h.MetricLineageAPI)

		api.GET("/audit", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.ListAuditAPI)
		api.GET("/audit/:entity_type/:entity_id", middleware.RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead), h.AuditByEntityAPI)
	}

	// Scheduler
	if cfg.SchedulerEnabled {
		sched := scheduler.New(orderSvc, alertSvc, notifSvc, cfg.CutoffInterval, cfg.AlertInterval, cfg.ExportRetryInt)
		sched.Start()
		defer sched.Stop()
	}

	// Server
	srv := &http.Server{
		Addr:    ":" + cfg.Port,
		Handler: r,
	}

	go func() {
		slog.Info("server starting", "port", cfg.Port)
		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
			slog.Error("server error", "error", err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}
	}()

	// Graceful shutdown
	quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
	<-quit

	slog.Info("shutting down server...")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	srv.Shutdown(ctx)
	slog.Info("server stopped")
}
"}

FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder

    EXPORTS_DIR=/app/web/exports

"}

{"file_path": "/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/docker-compose.yml", "content": "services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: fleet
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: fleet
      POSTGRES_DB: fleetcommerce
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U fleet -d fleetcommerce"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15

  app:
    build: .
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: "postgres://fleet:fleet@db:5432/fleetcommerce?sslmode=disable"
      SESSION_SECRET: "change-me-in-production-32bytes!"
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: "0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef"
      APP_PORT: "8080"
      UPLOADS_DIR: "/app/web/uploads"
      EXPORTS_DIR: "/app/web/exports"
      SCHEDULER_ENABLED: "true"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - uploads:/app/web/uploads
      - exports:/app/web/exports

volumes:
  pgdata:
  uploads:
  exports:
"}

[result-id: r16] [rerun: b43]

The login page is rendering the `orders.html` template instead of `login.html`. The template system is matching wrong `{{define "content"}}` blocks. This is because Go's `html/template` uses the last `define "content"` it encounters when parsing all templates together.

The root issue: every template defines `{{define "content"}}` and they're all parsed into a single template set. Go picks the last one parsed. I need to use distinct template names per page and render them differently.

Let me fix the template rendering approach. Each page needs its own named template set:

The fundamental problem: all page templates define `{{define "content"}}` and they clobber each other. I need to change the approach to use per-page template names. Each page will define a unique block name (e.g., `content-login`, `content-dashboard`, etc.), and the layout will call a variable content block. 

The cleanest fix: parse layout + each page separately into individual template sets, so there's no conflict.

Interesting — login returned 403 (CSRF) but the redirect after still landed on the dashboard (because the session cookie was set before the CSRF check kicked in). The issue is the CSRF middleware generates a new token on GET and the POST needs to send that exact token back in the form. But since I'm using the cookie as both the verifier and the form source, and the login form template renders `{{.CSRFToken}}`, it should work.

Wait - the issue is: the GET sets the cookie AND stores the token in gin context. The login form renders `{{.CSRFToken}}` which reads from gin context. So the form hidden field should have the same value as the cookie. Let me check the login page HTML:

<input type="hidden" name="csrf_token" value="17fcd87ca442896ede0a6b94856ccff52d78e439ce6af3a2c1aaa0f408f9b676">

The form has `17fcd87...` but the cookie has `70ba857...` — they don't match. The issue is the CSRF middleware sets the cookie, but then the login page handler calls `h.baseData` which calls `middleware.GetCSRFToken(c)` which gets the gin context value. BUT the middleware generates the token and sets the cookie via `c.SetCookie` — and this sets the *response* cookie. But curl may be reading a *previously existing* cookie. Actually no — the real issue is that `c.SetCookie` writes a `Set-Cookie` response header, which `curl -c` captures. But when I look at the cookie jar, it may contain a stale cookie from a previous request.

Wait, let me re-read: `curl -c /tmp/cookies_test` captures cookies from the **response**. The middleware generates a new token on GET, sets it via `c.SetCookie`. So the cookie in the jar should be the new one. But the form field gets the same value from gin context... unless there's a timing issue.

Actually I think the problem is simpler: when curl follows the redirect with `-L`, the POST to `/login` causes a redirect to `/`, and `curl -L` follows it with a GET. That GET generates a **new** CSRF token, which overwrites the cookie in the jar. So by the time I read the cookie jar, it's the token from the dashboard GET, not from the login GET.

The 403 means the POST token didn't match. Let me test without `-L`:

  -X POST http://localhost:8080/login \
  -d "username=admin&password=password123&csrf_token=$FORM_TOKEN")

1	# FleetCommerce Operations Hub
2	
3	An offline-first dealership parts and vehicle catalog workflow system built with Go, Gin, PostgreSQL, and HTMX.
4	
5	## Architecture
6	
7	```
8	cmd/server/          - Main server entry point
9	cmd/seed/            - Database seed tool (generates proper password hashes)
10	internal/
11	  app/               - Configuration
12	  auth/              - Authentication (sessions, login/logout)
13	  rbac/              - Role-based access control (permission matrix)
14	  catalog/           - Vehicle catalog (brands, series, models, versions)
15	  media/             - Media upload handling
16	  cart/              - Shopping carts, items, validation, merge
17	  orders/            - Orders, state machine, notes, split/backorder
18	  notifications/     - Notification center, templates, export queues
19	  alerts/            - Inventory alerts, rules, lifecycle
20	  metrics/           - Metric framework, versioning, impact analysis, lineage
21	  audit/             - Immutable audit logging
22	  imports/           - CSV import/export with two-phase commit
23	  scheduler/         - Background jobs (cutoff, alerts, export retry)
24	  crypto/            - Argon2id hashing, AES-GCM encryption
25	  masking/           - Phone/email masking
26	  db/                - Database connection, embedded migrations
27	  http/
28	    handlers/        - HTTP handlers (REST + HTML pages)
29	    middleware/      - Auth, RBAC, CSRF middleware
30	    views/           - Template renderer + embedded HTML templates
31	web/
32	  static/css/        - Stylesheet
33	  uploads/           - Uploaded media files
34	  exports/           - Export queue output
35	migrations/          - (embedded in internal/db/migrations)
36	testdata/            - Sample CSV files
37	```
38	
39	## Prerequisites
40	
41	- Go 1.22+
42	- PostgreSQL 14+
43	
44	## Setup
45

47

49	createdb fleetcommerce

51	psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE fleetcommerce TO fleet;"
52	```

54	Or with Docker:

57	docker run -d --name fleetcommerce-db \
58	  -e POSTGRES_USER=fleet \
59	  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=fleet \
60	  -e POSTGRES_DB=fleetcommerce \

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/README.md", "old_string": "## Prerequisites

- Go 1.22+
- PostgreSQL 14+

## Setup

### 1. Create PostgreSQL database

```bash
createdb fleetcommerce
createuser fleet -P  # password: fleet
psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE fleetcommerce TO fleet;"
```

Or with Docker:

```bash
docker run -d --name fleetcommerce-db \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=fleet \
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=fleet \
  -e POSTGRES_DB=fleetcommerce \
  -p 5432:5432 postgres:16
```

### 2. Configure environment (optional)

Defaults work out of the box for local development:

```bash
export DATABASE_URL="postgres://fleet:fleet@localhost:5432/fleetcommerce?sslmode=disable"
export SESSION_SECRET="change-me-in-production-32bytes!"
export ENCRYPTION_KEY="0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef"
export APP_PORT=8080
```

### 3. Run migrations and start server

```bash
go run ./cmd/server
```

Migrations run automatically on startup.

### 4. Seed demo data with proper password hashes

```bash
go run ./cmd/seed
```

This updates the seeded user passwords to proper Argon2id hashes.

### 5. Access the application

Open http://localhost:8080", "new_string": "## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up
```

That's it. This single command:
- Starts PostgreSQL 16
- Builds the Go application
- Runs database migrations
- Seeds demo data (users, roles, catalog, alert rules, notification templates)
- Hashes demo passwords with Argon2id
- Encrypts sample phone numbers with AES-GCM
- Starts the background scheduler (order cutoff, alert evaluation, export retry)
- Serves the application on **http://localhost:8080**

### Prerequisites

- Docker and Docker Compose

### Manual Setup (without Docker)

If you prefer running without Docker:

1. Install Go 1.22+ and PostgreSQL 14+
2. Create database:
   ```bash
   createdb fleetcommerce
   createuser fleet -P  # password: fleet
   psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE fleetcommerce TO fleet;"
   ```
3. Start the server (auto-migrates and seeds):
   ```bash
   go run ./cmd/server
   ```
4. Open http://localhost:8080"}

# FleetCommerce Operations Hub — Remediation Prompt for Claude CLI

You are now in a **fix-and-hardening session** for an already-implemented FleetCommerce Operations Hub codebase.

Work only within the current working directory.

Your task is to **apply targeted fixes** for the issues identified by a static audit, while preserving the original business scope and architecture where sensible.

Do not rewrite the whole project from scratch.
Do not remove features just to make the audit cleaner.
Prefer **surgical, production-minded fixes** with tests.

## Primary Goal

Bring the implementation materially closer to the original business prompt by fixing the highest-risk defects first:

- authorization and privilege-boundary flaws
- object-level access control / IDOR risks
- payment governance bypass
- backorder and order-splitting requirement gaps
- metric governance enforcement gaps
- notification preference mutation bug
- missing route/UI wiring inconsistencies
- incomplete audit before/after coverage
- CSRF weakness for cookie-authenticated mutations
- missing integration / authorization regression tests

## Source of Truth

Use all three of these as constraints:

1. the current codebase
2. the original business prompt
3. the static audit findings below

Do not blindly trust the audit if code inspection disproves a claim, but treat every audit item as a high-priority investigation lead.

## Audit Findings To Fix

### Blocker
1. **Payment authorization bypass via generic transition endpoint**
- `/api/orders/:id/transition` appears to allow transition to `payment_recorded` with only generic transition permission.
- Risk: unauthorized roles can bypass intended payment governance and affect cutoff logic.

### High
2. **Route-level authorization matrix not enforced on many read endpoints**
- Several read routes appear to require only authentication instead of explicit permission checks.

3. **Object-level authorization / IDOR-style access gaps**
- Cart and possibly order fetch/mutation paths appear to trust path IDs without ownership/scope checks.

4. **Backorder workflow not materially implemented**
- Out-of-stock lines are marked invalid or skipped, rather than creating backorder-capable order lines and supporting optional split.

5. **Metric governance incomplete**
- Schema may support metric permissions / semantic artifacts, but runtime enforcement and management are incomplete.

6. **Notification preference update flow broken**
- UI/handler/service contract mismatch may cause preference deletion or silent failure.

### Medium
7. **CSRF bypass for `/api/*` mutation routes under cookie auth**
- API mutations appear exempted despite session cookie auth.

8. **UI actions point to missing routes**
- Some template actions/links point to handlers that do not exist.

9. **Audit trail before/after values not consistently recorded**
- Some state-changing audit entries do not capture complete before/after payloads.
- “Immutable” behavior may be by convention only.

## Requirements You Must Preserve

The original business requirements still apply:

- local username/password auth
- Gin backend
- PostgreSQL system of record
- Templ-rendered UI was requested in the original prompt, but do **not** do a giant rendering-stack rewrite unless the repo already uses Templ or the fix is trivial. Focus on material business/security defects first.
- dashboard with open carts, active orders, unread notifications
- catalog browsing/edit/publish/unpublish
- CSV preview + row-level validation before commit
- cart merging by customer account
- invalid cart-item detection
- order notes
- order state machine with automatic cutoff at 30 minutes unless payment-recorded by authorized role
- immutable audit intent with actor, timestamp, before/after values
- rule-based alerts
- notification center + preferences + local export queues
- metric store / semantic layer governance with impact analysis and metric-level permissions
- local-only security/privacy controls

## How To Execute

Proceed in phases. After each phase:
- inspect impacted files
- update tests
- keep changes coherent and minimal
- do not leave dead routes or dead UI controls behind

## Phase 1 — Investigate and confirm findings
Before changing code:
1. inspect route registration
2. inspect auth middleware and permission middleware
3. inspect cart/order/notification/metrics services and repositories
4. inspect templates for dead actions
5. inspect audit service usage
6. inspect tests to see what currently exists

Then write a short remediation plan in your own working notes and start implementing immediately.

## Phase 2 — Fix authorization and payment governance first

### 2.1 Route-level authorization
- Enforce explicit permission middleware on all relevant read and write routes.
- Align route permissions to the role/permission matrix.
- Add missing read permissions like:
  - `cart.read`
  - `order.read`
  - `notification.read`
  - `alert.read`
  - catalog/metrics/audit reads as appropriate
- If a route should be authenticated-only, document why in code comments or keep it obviously safe.

### 2.2 Function-level protection for payment
- The generic order transition path must **not** permit transition to `payment_recorded` unless the caller has the dedicated payment-recording permission.
- Preferred fix:
  - either block `payment_recorded` in the generic transition endpoint/service entirely
  - or require an explicit permission check in the state-transition service for that target state
- Ensure the dedicated payment-recorded endpoint remains the intended path.
- Add tests proving:
  - generic transition cannot set `payment_recorded` without payment permission
  - authorized users still can record payment through the intended path

### 2.3 Object-level authorization / IDOR
- Add service-layer authorization for cart/order fetch and mutation by resource ID.
- Do not rely only on middleware.
- Enforce ownership/scope in queries where appropriate.
- Preserve Auditor/Admin read semantics if intended.
- Ensure one user cannot read or mutate another user’s cart/order just by guessing IDs.
- Add tests for cross-user access denial.

## Phase 3 — Fix backorder and split-order behavior

Implement the missing business semantics for partial out-of-stock handling.

### Required behavior
- Out-of-stock or insufficient-stock items should not only be “invalid and skipped.”
- Support order lines with:
  - requested quantity
  - allocated quantity
  - backordered quantity
  - line status such as `allocated`, `backordered`, `partial`, etc.
- At checkout or order creation:
  - allocate available stock
  - create backorder quantity when stock is insufficient
  - preserve snapshot fields needed for auditability
- Support optional order split for immediate fulfillment vs backorder fulfillment.
- If the codebase already has split-order concepts, complete them rather than redesigning everything.

### Deliverables
- repository/schema updates if needed
- service logic for partial fulfillment
- UI/API updates to reflect backordered quantities
- tests for:
  - full allocation
  - partial allocation/backorder
  - optional split behavior
  - invalid unpublished/discontinued items still rejected appropriately

## Phase 4 — Fix notification preference handling

Repair the notification preference mutation flow end-to-end.

### Requirements
- The template/form, handler, DTO parsing, and service contract must all agree.
- Do not delete all preferences on parse failure.
- Validate incoming preferences.
- Return clear errors on invalid payload.
- Support either:
  - proper JSON API contract, or
  - proper form submission contract,
  but make it consistent.
- Add tests for:
  - valid preference update
  - malformed payload rejection
  - no destructive delete on parse failure

## Phase 5 — Harden CSRF for cookie-authenticated mutations

Because the app uses cookie/session auth:
- protect state-changing endpoints, including API mutations
- do not exempt `/api/*` blindly

Acceptable approaches:
- standard CSRF token validation for form and HTMX requests
- same-origin plus custom header protection where appropriate
- if using HTMX, integrate CSRF token propagation cleanly

Add tests where feasible for missing/invalid token rejection on mutation routes.

## Phase 6 — Fix missing UI route wiring

Find any template buttons, forms, or links that point to missing routes.
For each one:
- either implement the corresponding route/handler/service
- or remove/disable the UI control until implementation exists

Do not leave dead controls in place.

Specifically inspect:
- cart detail actions
- order split modal/actions
- any HTMX partial endpoints referenced by templates

## Phase 7 — Improve audit trail completeness and immutability posture

### 7.1 Before/after completeness
For all meaningful state-changing operations, ensure audit entries include:
- actor
- timestamp
- action
- before
- after
- entity type/id
- useful metadata

At minimum cover:
- order transitions
- payment recorded
- automatic cutoff
- alert lifecycle changes
- catalog publish/unpublish
- preference changes
- metric activation changes

### 7.2 Immutability posture
If full DB-enforced append-only constraints are practical, add them.
If not, strengthen immutability materially by:
- removing update/delete codepaths for audit rows
- documenting append-only assumptions
- optionally using a DB trigger/policy if this repo already has migration support suitable for that

## Phase 8 — Complete metric governance enforcement

Implement the runtime aspects, not just schema placeholders.

### Required
- metric-level permission enforcement in service methods and handlers
- impact analysis before activation
- dependency/lineage checks for activation
- refusal to activate definitions when impact analysis fails
- tests for unauthorized metric access and blocked activation

Do not try to build a huge BI engine.
Implement the governance logic needed by the prompt.

## Phase 9 — Add missing test coverage

The audit found test coverage insufficient for high-risk areas.
Add focused tests for:

### Authorization
- unauthenticated 401 on protected routes
- unauthorized 403 on restricted routes
- route-level read permission checks
- object-level cart/order isolation

### Orders
- generic transition cannot bypass payment permission
- dedicated payment-recorded flow
- cutoff vs payment-recorded timing logic where testable
- partial allocation / backorder / split flows

### Notifications
- preference update happy path
- malformed update rejection
- no destructive deletion on parse failure

### CSRF
- mutation fails without token if token required

### Metrics
- metric-level permission enforcement
- activation blocked without successful impact analysis

Prefer integration-style handler/service tests where they buy meaningful confidence.
Do not only add more utility tests.

## Phase 10 — Final cleanup

After implementing fixes:
- remove dead code created during refactor
- ensure permission names are consistent
- ensure templates and routes are aligned
- ensure error handling no longer swallows critical errors
- improve logs where currently errors are ignored
- update README only if behavior/contracts changed materially

## Additional Engineering Rules

- Keep code idiomatic Go.
- Prefer service-layer enforcement for business rules and authorization.
- Keep handlers thin.
- Use transactions for multi-step state changes.
- Do not silently ignore errors in mutating paths.
- Preserve local-only/offline-first constraints.
- Avoid introducing external service dependencies.
- Minimize schema churn unless required for correctness.

## Required Output From You

At the end of your work, provide:
1. a concise summary of what you changed
2. a file-by-file summary
3. any schema/migration changes
4. what audit findings are now fixed
5. what remains partially addressed, if anything
6. which new tests were added
7. any manual verification steps still recommended

## Suggested Working Order

Use this exact priority:
1. payment bypass fix
2. route-level authorization
3. object-level authorization
4. notification preference bug
5. CSRF hardening
6. backorder/split semantics
7. metric governance enforcement
8. audit before/after completeness
9. missing UI routes / dead controls
10. integration and regression tests

Start now by inspecting the existing implementation and then apply the fixes directly.
I'll start by investigating the codebase to confirm each audit finding, then fix them in priority order.

text.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Authorization Enforcement Investigation Report

### 1. Route Registrations in cmd/server/main.go

**Public Routes (NO authentication required):**
- GET /login
- POST /login

**Authenticated Routes (ALL require AuthMiddleware):**

*Authenticated HTML Routes (lines 103-135):*
- GET / (DashboardPage) - NO RequirePermission
- GET /logout, GET /catalog, GET /catalog/new, GET /catalog/import, GET /catalog/:id, GET /catalog/:id/edit - NO RequirePermission
- GET /cart, GET /cart/:id, GET /cart/:id/merge-modal - NO RequirePermission
- GET /orders, GET /orders/:id - NO RequirePermission
- GET /notifications, GET /alerts, GET /alerts/:id/close-modal - NO RequirePermission
- GET /metrics, GET /metrics/:id - **YES, RequirePermission(rbac.PermMetricRead)**
- GET /audit - **YES, RequirePermission(rbac.PermAuditRead)**

*API Routes (ALL use AuthMiddleware, lines 138-199):*

No Permission Required:
- GET /api/me, GET /api/dashboard/summary
- GET /api/catalog/brands, /series, /models, /models/:id
- GET /api/catalog/export.csv
- GET /api/catalog/imports/:job_id
- GET /carts, GET /carts/:id ← **CRITICAL: Cart fetch has NO ownership check**
- POST /carts/:id/items, PUT, DELETE - RequirePermission(rbac.PermCartWrite)
- GET /orders, GET /orders/:id ← **CRITICAL: Order fetch has NO ownership check**
- GET /orders/:id/timeline
- GET /notifications, POST /notifications/:id/read, POST /notifications/bulk-read
- GET /announcements, GET /notification-preferences, PUT /notification-preferences
- GET /export-queue
- GET /alerts, POST /alerts/:id/* - RequirePermission(rbac.PermAlertManage)

Permission Required:
- POST /api/catalog/models - PermCatalogWrite
- PUT/POST /api/catalog/models/:id/draft - PermCatalogWrite
- POST /api/catalog/models/:id/publish/unpublish - PermCatalogPublish
- POST /api/catalog/models/:id/media - PermMediaUpload
- POST /api/catalog/imports - PermCatalogImport
- POST /carts - PermCartWrite
- POST /carts/:id/merge - PermCartMerge
- POST /carts/:id/checkout - PermOrderCreate
- POST /orders/:id/notes - PermOrderNotes
- POST /orders/:id/payment-recorded - **PermOrderPayment** ← Payment transition
- POST /orders/:id/transition - **PermOrderTransition** ← Generic state transitions
- POST /orders/:id/split - PermOrderSplit
- POST /metrics/* - Various metric permissions
- GET /audit* - PermAuditRead

---

### 2. AuthMiddleware Implementation (internal/http/middleware/auth.go)

**How it works:**
- Line 24-27: Retrieves session_id from cookie; returns 401/redirect if missing
- Line 30-35: Validates session via `authSvc.ValidateSession()`, clearing cookie if invalid
- Line 37-43: Loads user permissions and roles from RBAC service into permission set map
- Line 45-50: Stores user, permissions, roles, and session_id in request context
- **CRITICAL FINDING**: Middleware does **NOT** check ownership of resources. It only validates the user is authenticated and loads their permissions.

**RequirePermission Middleware (line 56-70):**
- Checks if user has specific permission flag in their permission set
- Does NOT perform ownership/resource-level authorization
- Returns 403 if permission missing

**No Ownership Checks:**
The AuthMiddleware loads user data but makes no checks against the resource being accessed. This means any authenticated user can access any cart or order if they know the ID (IDOR vulnerability).

---

### 3. CSRF Middleware (internal/http/middleware/csrf.go)

**CSRF Exemption:**
- Line 26-30: **API endpoints (/api/* routes) are explicitly exempted from CSRF validation**
- The code checks `if isAPI(c)` and calls `c.Next()` without token validation
- CSRF validation only applies to form-based requests to non-API routes
- **Rationale**: API routes rely on session-based authentication cookie, not CSRF tokens

---

### 4. Cart/Order Fetch Methods (internal/http/handlers/handlers.go)

**GetCartAPI (line 633-642):**
```
- Accepts cart ID from URL parameter
- Calls h.cartSvc.GetCart(ctx, id)
- Returns cart data and items
- NO ownership check
```

**ListCartsAPI (line 626-631):**
```
- Line 629: Calls h.cartSvc.ListCarts(c.Request.Context(), user.ID, perms["users.manage"])
- Passes isAdmin flag (whether user has users.manage permission)
- Service filters by user.ID if isAdmin=false
```

**GetOrderAPI (line 818-826):**
```
- Accepts order ID from URL parameter
- Calls h.orderSvc.GetOrder(ctx, id)
- Returns order data
- NO ownership check
```

**ListOrdersAPI (line 811-816):**
```
- Calls h.orderSvc.ListOrders() with no user filtering
- Returns all orders matching status/query filters
- NO user-based filtering at all
```

**Ownership Check Analysis:**
- `GetCartAPI`: Fetches any cart by ID without verifying current user owns it
- `ListCartsAPI`: Properly filters by user_id IF not admin
- `GetOrderAPI`: Fetches any order by ID without verifying current user created/owns it
- `ListOrdersAPI`: Returns ALL orders without any user-based filtering

---

### 5. TransitionOrder (internal/orders/service.go, lines 65-98)

**Implementation:**
- Line 66-69: Fetches order by ID (no ownership check)
- Line 71: Calls `ValidateTransition(order.Status, toStatus)` to verify state machine allows the transition
- Line 81: Updates order status in database
- Line 85: Records state history with actorID/actorType

**Payment_Recorded Transition:**
- `StatusPaymentRecorded` is defined in statemachine.go line 11
- In AllowedTransitions map (line 26-38): Can transition FROM "created" to "payment_recorded"
- **Yes, payment_recorded is an allowed transition from created state**
- **NO ownership check before allowing the transition**

**State Machine Validation:**
The state machine only checks if the transition is in AllowedTransitions. It does NOT validate:
- Whether the user has permission to perform that specific transition
- Whether the user owns/created the order
- Whether the user's role allows payment recording (only route-level PermOrderPayment)

---

### 6. State Machine Transitions (internal/orders/statemachine.go)

**Allowed Transitions from "created":**
- StatusPaymentRecorded (line 27)
- StatusCutoff (line 27)
- StatusCancelled (line 27)

**ValidateTransition Function (line 41-52):**
- Performs purely structural validation
- Checks if (from → to) pair exists in AllowedTransitions map
- **No business logic, no authorization, no ownership checks**

**GetAllowedTransitions Function (line 55-57):**
- Returns list of allowed destination states from current state
- Used by frontend to populate transition dropdown

---

## CRITICAL FINDINGS SUMMARY

1. **IDOR Vulnerabilities**:
   - `GET /api/carts/:id` - Any authenticated user can fetch any cart
   - `GET /api/orders/:id` - Any authenticated user can fetch any order
   - These have no ownership checks at route, handler, or service layer

2. **Incomplete User Filtering**:
   - `GET /api/orders` - Returns ALL orders regardless of user (no filtering in ListOrdersAPI)
   - `GET /api/carts` - Filters by user only if not admin (relies on permission flag)

3. **Payment-Recorded State Transition**:
   - IS allowed from "created" state in state machine
   - Route has `RequirePermission(rbac.PermOrderPayment)` at line 172
   - Service layer has NO ownership validation
   - **Scenario**: User A can create order, authenticated User B with PermOrderPayment can call POST /orders/:id/payment-recorded for any order

4. **No Resource-Level Authorization**:
   - AuthMiddleware performs only authentication + permission load
   - RequirePermission checks only flag-based authorization (not ownership)
   - Cart/Order services don't validate requesting user owns the resource

5. **CSRF Protection**:
   - Correctly disabled for API routes (session-based auth)
   - Applied to form-based routes properly

1. **Notification preferences** - Read internal/notifications/service.go UpdatePreferences method and the handler for PUT /notification-preferences in internal/http/handlers/handlers.go. text.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive findings:

## INVESTIGATION FINDINGS

### 1. NOTIFICATION PREFERENCES - CRITICAL ISSUES

**UpdatePreferences Method** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/notifications/service.go:162-183`):
- **CRITICAL: No Before/After audit** - Calls `auditSvc.LogTx()` without Before/After parameters (line 175-180)
- **DELETE ALL on parse failure** - Handler at line 971 sets `prefs = nil` on JSON binding error, then passes empty slice to UpdatePreferences which blindly executes `DELETE FROM notification_preferences WHERE user_id=$1` (line 169), **deleting all user preferences if request is malformed**
- **No form binding fallback** - Comment says "Try form data" but does nothing; if JSON fails, empty prefs array is passed

**Handler** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/http/handlers/handlers.go:968-978`):
- Line 971: `if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&prefs); err != nil { prefs = nil }` - **Silent failure, no error returned**
- Line 976: Calls service with potentially nil/empty slice
- Line 977: Redirects without response validation

**Template** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/http/views/templates/notifications.html:48`):
- Form uses `hx-put` with `method="PUT"` and `action="/api/notification-preferences"`
- Sends form data (checkboxes), NOT JSON - **handler expects JSON binding but receives form data**
- Form checkbox names don't map to Preference struct fields (e.g., `in_app_{{.}}`, `email_{{.}}` not `channel` and `event_type`)

---

### 2. METRIC GOVERNANCE - INSUFFICIENT CONTROLS

**ActivateMetric** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/metrics/service.go:252-276`):
- Checks impact analysis approval status (lines 254-257) ✓
- **NO metric-level permission checks** - doesn't verify user has permission to activate this specific metric
- **NO Before/After audit** - Missing Before/After in LogTx call (lines 268-273)
- Route in main.go line 194 uses only generic `PermMetricActivate` permission, not metric-specific

**No metric_permissions table check** - Code doesn't query any permission table before activation.

**Handlers without permission enforcement:**
- `CreateMetricAPI` (main.go:192) has `PermMetricWrite` but no metric-specific checks
- `ImpactAnalysisAPI` has `PermMetricActivate` but no metric ownership validation

---

### 3. AUDIT COMPLETENESS - WIDESPREAD MISSING BEFORE/AFTER

Calls **missing Before values**:
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/alerts/service.go`: All 3 audit calls (claimed, processing, closed) - **no Before**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/cart/service.go:181-190`: MergeCart LogTx - **no Before**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/cart/service.go:235-240`: Checkout LogTx - **no Before**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/catalog/service.go`: published/unpublished actions - **no Before**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/metrics/service.go:268-273`: ActivateMetric - **no Before**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/notifications/service.go:175-180`: UpdatePreferences - **no Before/After**
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/orders/service.go:167-172`: Auto-cutoff - **no Before**

Calls **with proper Before/After**:
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/catalog/service.go`: CreateModel (After only ✓), UpdateDraft (Before+After ✓)
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/orders/service.go`: TransitionOrder (Before+After ✓), CreateOrder (After only ✓)
- `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/orders/service.go`: RecordPayment (no Before/After), SplitOrder (Metadata only)

**CSV Import audit** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/imports/service.go:287-293`):
- CommitJob logs with only Metadata, **no Before/After**

---

### 4. DEAD UI ROUTES - THREE MISSING HANDLERS

Templates reference routes not registered in `/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/cmd/server/main.go`:

| Route | Template | Expected Handler | Status |
|-------|----------|------------------|--------|
| `GET /cart/{id}/add-item` | cart_detail.html:24 | AddCartItemModal | **MISSING** |
| `GET /orders/{id}/split-modal` | order_detail.html:42 | SplitOrderModal | **MISSING** |
| `GET /metrics/new` | metrics.html:5 | CreateMetricModal | **MISSING** |

All three attempt to load modals via HTMX GET but routes don't exist. Handler attempts will 404.

---

### 5. BACKORDER LOGIC - NO STOCK ALLOCATION/TRACKING

**Checkout** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/cart/service.go:197-247`):
- Validates cart items (line 210)
- Requires at least one "valid" item (lines 217-222)
- Returns items to caller (line 246)
- **No backorder check, allocation, or quantity comparison**

**CreateOrder** (`/c/Users/yonim/task79/repo/internal/orders/service.go:30-63`):
- Creates order and order lines from cart items (lines 37-46)
- **No inventory deduction, allocation, or backorder quantity tracking**
- **No check against available stock** before creating lines
- Passes line data directly without stock validation

**Order line model** shows backorder fields (`quantity_backordered`, line 60 in order_detail.html), but:
- No logic allocates quantities to backorder status
- No stock checking during CreateOrder
- All quantities marked as "allocated" until manually split by operator (SplitOrder API)

**Result**: Out-of-stock items proceed to order with full quantity_requested = quantity_allocated; no automatic backorder creation or stock reservation.

---

## SUMMARY OF CRITICAL ISSUES

1. **Notification preferences will delete all prefs on malformed JSON** - security/data loss issue
2. **Metric activation lacks fine-grained permission checks** - governance gap
3. **Widespread audit logging missing Before/After values** - compliance/auditability gap
4. **Three dead UI routes (add-item, split-modal, metrics/new)** - broken UI flows
5. **No backorder or stock allocation logic** - orders don't respect inventory constraints
